hero.pill: A research company building the physical layer of 6G
hero.title: We build wireless systems that think.
hero.body: Source Sequence is a deep-tech research company developing AI-native radio architectures for the 6G era. Founded in Hangzhou by researchers trained at leading universities across China, the UK, and the US.
hero.modelCaption: Pinching Antenna Systems · One of our active research lines

vision.label: 01 / Vision
vision.body: For decades, antennas have been static—fixed shapes radiating fixed patterns. We believe that as wireless systems enter the 6G era, the physical layer itself must become intelligent, adaptive, and software-defined.
vision.quote: We build radio systems where every photon is placed by an algorithm.

pass.title: Pinching Antenna Systems
pass.desc: Reconfigurable radiation along low-loss dielectric waveguides. Our flagship research line, exploring on-demand line-of-sight wireless coverage for indoor and immersive environments.

control.title: AI Control Plane
control.desc: Real-time decision-making for wireless physical-layer reconfiguration. Neural agents that orchestrate beams, links, and apertures at sub-millisecond latency.

generative.title: Generative RF Design
generative.desc: Neural topology search for antennas, waveguides, and metasurfaces. We use diffusion models and neural architecture search to design RF structures beyond human intuition.

isac.title: Integrated Sensing & Communication
isac.desc: One aperture, two functions. Antennas that simultaneously connect and perceive—a foundational primitive for 6G.

aircomp.title: AirComp & Wireless AI Compute
aircomp.desc: Treating the wireless channel itself as a computing substrate. We explore over-the-air computation for distributed AI inference at the edge of 6G networks.

footer.copyright: © 2026 Source Sequence Technology · Hangzhou · All rights reserved

careers.label: 07 / Careers
careers.quote: We're building an unusual company. If you want to build the physical layer of 6G, talk to us.
careers.intro: We hire researchers, engineers, and operators who care about doing fundamental work. Open positions are posted as we grow—but we're always open to talking with exceptional people.

rfEngineer.title: Antenna / RF Research Engineer
rfEngineer.desc: Lead the design and characterization of next-generation dielectric waveguide systems.

aiResearcher.title: AI Researcher · Wireless Physical Layer
aiResearcher.desc: Develop neural architectures for real-time control of reconfigurable radio systems.

frontendEngineer.title: Frontend / Visualization Engineer
frontendEngineer.desc: Build the tools and interfaces that make our research legible to the world.

publications.label: 06 / Publications — We publish what we build.

paper.title: Core Views on AI-Native Radio
paper.abstract: Why we believe the physical layer of wireless communication is the next frontier for AI—and why the 6G era requires us to rebuild radio systems from first principles.

publications.comingSoon: More papers and technical notes coming soon.

about.label: 05 / About
about.heading: A small research company with global roots.
about.para1: Source Sequence was founded in Hangzhou in 2025 by a group of researchers and engineers trained at leading universities across China, the United Kingdom, and the United States. We came together around a simple thesis: that the physical layer of wireless communication—long treated as fixed, passive infrastructure—is the next frontier for AI.
about.para2: We are a research-first company. We publish what we build. We collaborate with universities and standards bodies. We believe the path to 6G runs through new physics, not just new software.

team.label: Team
team.value: Master's & PhD researchers from China, UK & US

factory.title: Smart Factory & Industrial IoT
factory.desc: Reconfigurable mmWave coverage in dynamic NLOS environments.

immersive.title: Immersive Spaces
immersive.desc: On-demand line-of-sight for XR, stadiums, and large exhibition venues.

hangar.title: Low-Altitude Hangars & Vertiports
hangar.desc: Antenna infrastructure for the emerging low-altitude economy.

lab.label: 03 / Lab
lab.heading: How we work.
lab.intro: We operate a lean wireless research lab in Hangzhou, combining in-house prototyping with simulation, open hardware, and university partnerships.

simulation.title: Simulation & Modeling
simulation.desc: Full-wave EM simulation, channel modeling, and RF system co-design.

hardware.title: Open Hardware Prototyping
hardware.desc: SDR-based over-the-air experimentation and rapid waveguide fabrication.

partners.title: Partner Lab Network
partners.desc: Collaborative access to mmWave testbeds and anechoic chambers through university partnerships in China, the UK, and the US.

aiStack.title: AI Compute Stack
aiStack.desc: GPU-accelerated training and simulation pipelines for RF neural models.
